message:
fix(serve/checkpoints): stream checkpoint export bodies instead of buffering in memory

handleExportCheckpoint() previously did
    data, err := os.ReadFile(tmpPath)
    ...
    w.Header().Set("Content-Length", strconv.Itoa(len(data)))
    w.Write(data)
which loads the entire export tarball into memory before writing
it to the response.  For multi-GB checkpoint exports that's a
straightforward OOM trigger on the server side, especially when
several clients are downloading checkpoints concurrently.

internal/serve/checkpoints.go

- Replace the os.ReadFile + w.Write(data) pair with:
    f, err := os.Open(tmpPath)         // open instead of read
    defer f.Close()
    info, err := f.Stat()              // stat for Content-Length
    w.Header().Set("Content-Length", strconv.FormatInt(info.Size(), 10))
    io.Copy(w, f)                      // stream
- Three error sites now distinguish "open" vs "stat" vs "write"
  failure cases:
    "failed to open export file" — surface to client as 500.
    "failed to stat export file" — surface to client as 500.
    write failure — log only, since the headers are already on
        the wire and we can't change the response status.
- Header order is preserved so clients that read Content-Length
  before draining the body still see the right size.
- io.Copy uses the runtime's default 32KB buffer; that's the
  same allocation pattern Go's net/http already uses internally
  for ServeFile, so memory ceiling is bounded regardless of
  export size.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
